propositions: Actions and tags in release.yml use moving major-version tags rather than immutable full-SHA pins, so two runs of the same source SHA can execute different build/publish code.
Release checkouts do not set persist-credentials: false, leaving the token persisted in local git config by default.

The MCP publisher is downloaded from a mutable releases/latest URL and executed without digest/signature verification in a job holding id-token: write, allowing an unverified binary to mint an OIDC token.
Build tools install with unrestricted upgrades and no hash-locked constraints, so the build environment (including Hatchling) is not reproducibly pinned.

The fix enforces generic action-security invariants via zizmor + actionlint scoped to release.yml under make lint, plus a thin test asserting rebar-specific invariants (--no-isolation, mcp_verify/mcp_registry split, chmod +x, evidence wiring, docs substrings).

The MCP publisher flow is split into an unprivileged mcp_verify job (download + sha256sum -c) and an OIDC-privileged mcp_registry job that consumes only the verified artifact, restoring the executable bit after download.
